idea-plugin.name: GenAI Capture
idea-plugin.description:
Capture and annotate screenshots without ever leaving your IDE. Grab the whole desktop, mark it up with a full annotation toolkit, then copy, save, or pin the result — in a single keystroke.

What you can do

  • Capture the full (multi-monitor) desktop instantly, after a countdown, or with the IDE hidden so you snap whatever is behind it.
  • Annotate with arrows, rectangles, ellipses, lines, freehand pen, numbered steps, text, highlighter, mosaic/blur redaction, and tiled watermarks.
  • Redact sensitive data with the mosaic tool before sharing.
  • Finish by copying to the clipboard, saving to disk, or pinning the shot as an always-on-top floating window for reference.

Entry points

  • Tools > Take Screenshot — capture now (⌘⇧1 / Ctrl+Shift+1), with a camera button in the status bar.
  • Tools > Capture Desktop (Hide IDE) — hide the IDE, capture the desktop behind it; the IDE stays hidden until you close the overlay (⌘⇧2 / Ctrl+Shift+2), with a monitor button in the status bar.
  • Tools > Take Delayed Screenshot > 3s / 5s / 10s — an on-screen countdown before the grab, so you can switch windows or open a menu first.
  • Every action also appears in Find Action and is rebindable in Settings > Keymap.

Settings (Settings > Tools > GenAI Capture)

  • Watermark — default text plus opacity, size, rotation, spacing, blur and colour, with a live preview; optionally stamp every capture automatically.
  • Toolbar — pick which annotation tools appear on the capture toolbar and drag to reorder them.

Why you'll like it

  • Stays in your flow — no context switch to a separate screenshot app.
  • Runs everywhere — works in every JetBrains IDE (IntelliJ IDEA, PyCharm, GoLand, WebStorm, PhpStorm, CLion, Rider, RubyMine, …).
  • Lightweight & private — pure Java/Swing, no external services or telemetry; captures never leave your machine.
  • Multi-monitor and HiDPI aware.

macOS: grant the IDE Screen Recording permission (System Settings > Privacy & Security > Screen Recording), or captures come back black — the same one-time grant any screen-capture tool needs.

action.description: Hide the IDE window, capture the desktop behind it, then restore it
